sprint: World Builder — Mandatory Sign-In + Versioned Cloud Sync + My Cloud Worlds
task: A real, confirmed data-loss incident -- traced via the user's own console diagnostics after 'why am not seeing my forest adventure in builder as am seeing it in studio' -- found builder_projects (Builder Project Cloud Backup) was 'local-primary, cloud backup only, never a second read path' with zero protection against two tabs/sessions/devices silently overwriting each other's saves. Three explicit user decisions implemented: (1) versioned, conflict-aware sync reusing the existing builder_projects.updated_at column -- push(project,opts.expectedUpdatedAt) now performs a conditional update, surfacing a genuine conflict as {ok:false,conflict:true,cloudUpdatedAt} rather than silently overwriting; new get(projectId) fetches the current cloud row. (2) A new 'My Cloud Worlds' Welcome-screen section listing every cloud backup this account owns, with per-row Sync to This Device / Update Available / Up to Date states. (3) Mandatory sign-in for World Builder only -- a new non-dismissible #wb-signin-gate (Sign In / Create Account, via a new themeRepositoryClient.js signUp()) blocks the Welcome screen until signed in; Studio's own signinless _ensureAuth() anonymous-session fallback is completely untouched (confirmed via git diff). openWorkspace() now runs a non-blocking _checkCloudFreshness() showing an explicit 'Newer Cloud Version Found' prompt when the cloud has moved since this device last synced; the Workspace's cloud-sync badge shows a real 'conflict' state with a force-overwrite escape hatch. Verified via a Node unit test against the real projectSync.js (13/13), and two full-browser Playwright suites mocking ThemeRepositoryClient/ProjectSync (15/15 and 8/8), zero console errors throughout. Both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2) pass unchanged; git diff --stat confirms zero changes to tools/world-builder/ (v1) or any Studio-side file. No supabase/schema.sql change needed -- the existing updated_at column already suffices. Confined to js/themeRepositoryClient.js (additive signUp only) and tools/world-builder-v2/{index.html,css/world-builder.css,js/worldBuilderApp.js,js/services/projectSync.js}.
